mnemonics: Order Matters Most: Catch kids before parents.
Specific to General: Start small, end big.
keyPoints: Catch blocks are evaluated in the order they are written.
More specific exception types (like ArithmeticException) must be caught before more general ones (like Exception).
Only one catch block will be executed for a single try block.
commonMistakes: Placing a catch block for a general exception (e.g., Exception) before a specific one, which makes the specific block unreachable.
Forgetting that a superclass catch block will also catch all of its subclasses.
